key_items: Bitcoin price stays under $77K as US bond yields near 20-year highs -- BTC pinned below $77K amid rising yields
Recent Bitcoin holders sell $770M BTC at a loss amid $65K BTC price calls -- Short-term holders capitulating with $65K targets
Bitcoin ETFs Shed $649M in a Day as Long-Term BTC Holders accumulate -- Mixed signals with institutional flows diverging
reasoning: Bitcoin faces significant near-term headwinds from elevated US bond yields (20-year highs) and technical weakness at $77K support level, with short-term holders capitulating and analysts calling for $65K-$70K targets. However, this is partially offset by long-term institutional accumulation (MSTR, Strategy, Strive buying heavily), SEC tokenization catalysts, and positive global regulatory developments (UK, Japan, SEC), which suggest support for a longer-term bullish thesis despite near-term volatility.
risks: Break below $76K support level could trigger cascading liquidations toward $70K-$65K as technical levels fail and short-term holder capitulation accelerates.
